title: Varnish Grace and Keep Serving Stale Content Frequency
dataSources: `index=proxy` `sourcetype=varnish:vsl` grace or HFM related VSL lines
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder, Splunk Connect for Syslog, or HTTP Event Collector; `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for listed sourcetypes
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="varnish:vsl"
| regex _raw="(?i)(grace|stale|hit-for-pass)"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count by host, _time
description: Stale serving shields origins but can confuse content owners if unexplained.
implementation: Tune thresholds against editorial SLOs; document VCL `grace`/`keep`.
visualization: Time series for rates and latency, top/dedup for hotspots, single-value alerts on health thresholds.
grandmaExplanation: We watch varnish grace and keep serving stale content frequency and catch issues early, before they turn into outages for the people who rely on the network.
knownFalsePositives: Edge cases: multi-vendor mixes and ad hoc feeds can include lab traffic and partial visibility that mimics issues until scopes are defined. Tuning tip: match this to «Varnish Grace and Keep Serving Stale Content Frequency» and exclude change windows, scans, and lab VLANs you already expect.
